description: Creator Marketplace — Enable users to create and sell cosmetics (avatar decorations, effects, nameplates, soundboard) with full upload, browse, purchase, and application flow.

userStories.title: Database schema — Cosmetics tables and enums
userStories.description: As a developer, I need database tables to store cosmetics, purchases, and creator analytics.
acceptanceCriteria: Add cosmetic_type enum: 'avatar_decoration' | 'effect' | 'nameplate' | 'soundboard'

Add cosmetics table: { id (uuid), creator_id (uuid FK users), name (text), description (text), type (cosmetic_type), preview_image_url (text), asset_url (text), price (integer cents), created_at, updated_at, is_published (boolean default false) }
Add cosmetic_purchases table: { id (uuid), user_id (uuid FK users), cosmetic_id (uuid FK cosmetics), purchased_at (timestamptz), is_equipped (boolean default false) }
Add database indexes on creator_id, type, is_published for query performance
Generate migration files and run successfully with no errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend — Cosmetics CRUD endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need backend APIs to create, list, update, and delete cosmetics.
acceptanceCriteria: Add POST /cosmetics endpoint: creates a draft cosmetic for the authenticated creator — body: { name, description, type, preview_image_url?, asset_url? } — returns cosmetic with is_published: false
Add GET /cosmetics/:id endpoint: returns cosmetic details including preview image and asset URLs
Add PATCH /cosmetics/:id endpoint: authenticated creator can update name, description, preview_image_url, asset_url, price, is_published — creator_id must match or 403
Add DELETE /cosmetics/:id endpoint: authenticated creator can delete unpublished cosmetics only — must own the cosmetic or 403
Add GET /cosmetics/creator/:creatorId endpoint: returns all published cosmetics by creator with pagination — used by marketplace browse
Add GET /users/@me/cosmetics endpoint: returns authenticated user's draft and published cosmetics
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend — Cosmetics purchase and equip endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need purchase and equip endpoints to handle cosmetic transactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add POST /cosmetics/:id/purchase endpoint: authenticated user purchases a cosmetic — deducts price from wallet, creates cosmetic_purchases record with is_equipped: false, returns success or insufficient_balance error

Add PATCH /cosmetics/:id/equip endpoint: authenticated user equips a cosmetic they own — sets is_equipped: true on their purchase record, can only equip one per type (e.g. one avatar_decoration at a time)
Add DELETE /cosmetics/:id/equip endpoint: unequips a cosmetic — sets is_equipped: false
Add GET /users/@me/equipped-cosmetics endpoint: returns array of currently equipped cosmetics by type { type, cosmetic_id, asset_url }
Wallet deduction is atomic — purchase fails entirely if wallet deduction fails
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend — Cosmetics image upload handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to handle image uploads for cosmetics (preview and assets).
acceptanceCriteria: Add POST /cosmetics/upload endpoint: multipart/form-data handler for preview_image and asset_image uploads — uses MinIO S3 to store files
Returns { preview_image_url, asset_url } — full URLs to the uploaded files
File size limits: 10MB for preview, 50MB for asset
Supported formats: PNG, JPEG for preview; PNG for asset (transparency support)
Files stored in MinIO with path structure: cosmetics/{cosmetic_id}/{timestamp}-{filename}
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Files: apps/api/src/modules/cosmetics/cosmetics.router.ts (add upload handler), use existing MinIO config

userStories.title: Frontend API client — Cosmetics namespace
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need API methods to call cosmetics endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Add api.cosmetics namespace in apps/web/src/lib/api.ts with methods: create, get, update, delete, listByCreator, listMyCosm etics, purchase, equip, unequip, getEquipped, upload
upload method: FormData POST to /cosmetics/upload, returns { preview_image_url, asset_url }
All methods use apiFetch with proper error handling
Purchase method revalidates user wallet after success
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend — Creator Dashboard page structure
userStories.description: As a creator, I want a dashboard to manage my cosmetics.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/pages/CreatorDashboardPage.tsx with two-tab layout: 'My Cosmetics' (draft + published) and 'Analytics'
My Cosmetics tab shows cards in grid: [Thumbnail] Name Type | Price | [Edit] [Delete] [Publish/Unpublish] [Stats]
+ Create New Cosmetic button opens a modal or navigates to /creator/cosmetics/new
Cosmetics list shows draft items first (grey badge 'Draft'), then published items
Filters: All Types, Avatar Decorations, Effects, Nameplates, Soundboard
Add route /creator/dashboard in App.tsx
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — Create/Edit Cosmetic form
userStories.description: As a creator, I want to upload and configure a cosmetic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/components/cosmetics/CosmeticForm.tsx reusable component (mode: 'create' | 'edit')

Form fields: Name (required, max 100), Description (optional, max 1000), Type dropdown (required), Price input (cents, optional for draft), Preview Image upload (drag/drop or file picker), Asset upload (drag/drop or file picker)
On file drop/select: shows preview thumbnail for images; shows file size + format validation inline
On Name blur: auto-generates URL-safe slug (used internally if needed)
Save as Draft button: saves without preview/asset URLs, allows later upload
Save & Publish button: requires all fields + preview + asset; uploads images to MinIO, then saves cosmetic as is_published: true
Success toast: 'Cosmetic published!' or 'Draft saved'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — Cosmetics Marketplace browse page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse cosmetics from all creators.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/pages/CosmeticsMarketplacePage.tsx
Grid of cosmetic cards: [Preview Image] Name Creator | Price | [View] or [Buy] or [Equipped] (based on ownership/equip state)
Filter bar: Type dropdown (All, Avatar Decorations, Effects, Nameplates, Soundboard), Sort dropdown (Newest, Most Popular, Price: Low→High, Price: High→Low)
Search bar: filters by cosmetic name
Pagination or infinite scroll loading
Creator avatar chip on each card with creator name link to /profile/:creatorId
Clicking card opens detail modal or navigates to /cosmetics/:id
Add route /cosmetics in App.tsx
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — Cosmetic detail modal / page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see full details of a cosmetic before buying.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/components/cosmetics/CosmeticDetailModal.tsx or page variant
Shows: large preview image, name, creator (clickable to profile), type badge, description, price in Gratonites
Preview section: if available, show asset in a themed preview (small mockup of avatar with decoration, or effect animation, etc.)
Call-to-action: [Buy Now] if user doesn't own it, [Equip] if owned but not equipped, [Unequip] if equipped, [Go to Creator] link to creator profile
Creator info section: creator name, cosmetics count, total sales count
Related cosmetics: show 3-4 other cosmetics by same creator or same type
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — Purchase flow and confirmation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to purchase a cosmetic with confirmation and balance check.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking [Buy Now] shows a purchase confirmation modal: cosmetic preview + name + price + current wallet balance + [Confirm Purchase] [Cancel]
On Confirm: calls api.cosmetics.purchase(cosmeticId)
Success: toast 'Cosmetic added to your collection!', modal closes, card updates to [Equip] state
Insufficient balance: shows error modal 'Not enough Gratonites. Upgrade your balance?' with link to /gratonite
Purchase is instant and atomic — wallet + cosmetic_purchases table both update or neither
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — Equip/Unequip cosmetics
userStories.description: As a user, I want to equip cosmetics to my profile.
acceptanceCriteria: In Cosmetics Marketplace or inventory: [Equip] button calls api.cosmetics.equip(cosmeticId)
On success: button changes to [Equipped], toast 'Cosmetic equipped!'
Clicking [Equipped]: shows [Unequip] option; unequip call removes the equipped state
One cosmetic per type at a time — if user tries to equip a second avatar_decoration, previous one auto-unequips with notification
Equipped cosmetics are also visible in user inventory page (/cosmetics/inventory)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — User cosmetics inventory page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all my owned cosmetics in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/pages/CosmeticsInventoryPage.tsx
Grid of cosmetic cards: [Preview] Name Type | [Equip] or [Equipped] or [Unequip] based on state
Filter by Type (All, Avatar Decorations, Effects, Nameplates, Soundboard)
Section at top: 'Equipped Cosmetics' showing currently active items by type
Add route /cosmetics/inventory in App.tsx
Link from user profile or settings to view own inventory
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — Apply cosmetics to avatar rendering
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my equipped cosmetics on my avatar.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify avatar rendering component (likely in UserCard, UserProfilePage, etc.) to fetch equipped cosmetics via api.cosmetics.getEquipped()
Avatar decorations: render as CSS overlay layers with position/scale based on cosmetic metadata
Effects: apply CSS animations or SVG filters from cosmetic asset
Nameplates: render as styled label above/below avatar
Soundboard: store reference but defer actual sound playback to later (US-014)
Cosmetics render in correct z-order and don't break layout
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — Cosmetics context store
userStories.description: As a developer, I need state management for equipped cosmetics.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/stores/cosmeticsStore.ts using Zustand with: equippedCosmetics: Cosmetic[], loadEquipped(), equip(cosmeticId), unequip(cosmeticId)
Initialize on app load or user login by fetching api.cosmetics.getEquipped()
Store is shared globally so avatars across the app can render consistent cosmetics
Update store on purchase/equip/unequip actions so UI is in sync
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend — Creator analytics — Sales count
userStories.description: As a creator, I want to see how many times my cosmetics sold.
acceptanceCriteria: Add GET /cosmetics/:id/stats endpoint: returns { total_sales, total_revenue_gratonites, created_at, updated_at }
Queries cosmetic_purchases table for cosmetic_id and counts rows
Creator can only view stats for their own cosmetics (check creator_id or 403)
Stats are cached in cosmetics row or computed on query (no separate analytics table for now)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend — Creator Dashboard Analytics tab
userStories.description: As a creator, I want to see sales and revenue analytics.
acceptanceCriteria: In CreatorDashboardPage Analytics tab: list all creator's cosmetics with columns: Name | Type | Sales | Revenue (Gratonites) | Avg Price
Each row is clickable to show detail: cosmetic preview, total sales count, cumulative revenue, sales trend graph (if data exists)
Summary card at top: Total Sales, Total Revenue, Avg Price per Cosmetic, Most Popular Cosmetic
Fetch stats via api.cosmetics.getStats(cosmeticId) for each cosmetic
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — Creator profile cosmetics showcase
userStories.description: As a user visiting a creator's profile, I want to see their published cosmetics.

Click cosmetic card to open detail modal (reuse CosmeticDetailModal from US-009)
Show total cosmetics count and total sales count in creator bio section
Link '[View All Cosmetics]' goes to /creator/:creatorId/cosmetics (dedicated creator shop page)

userStories.title: Frontend — Dedicated creator shop page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse all cosmetics from a specific creator.

Shows creator header: avatar, name, 'Follow'/'Unfollow' button, cosmetics count, sales count, bio snippet
Grid of creator's published cosmetics below
Filters: Type, Sort by
Empty state if creator has no published cosmetics

userStories.title: Integration — Add cosmetics link to main nav

userStories.title: Testing — Cosmetics purchase flow e2e
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I want to verify the full cosmetic purchase and equip flow works.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test file: apps/web/src/pages/CosmeticsMarketplacePage.test.tsx or e2e test

Test scenario: (1) User navigates to /cosmetics, (2) Clicks cosmetic card, (3) Clicks [Buy Now], (4) Confirms purchase, (5) Card updates to [Equip], (6) User navigates to inventory, (7) Cosmetic appears in list, (8) Click [Equip], (9) Cosmetic shows as equipped, (10) Avatar reflects cosmetic visually
All assertions should pass
Tests pass
